items.title: Wellington Is Passing on Data-Center Debt Deals on Property Value Concerns
items.description: Data-center debt risks are rising, there’s uncertainty over what the properties are worth and returns are falling, according to Wellington Management.

items.title: Tesla Short Sellers Rake In $4 Billion as Shares Nosedive
items.description: The deep plunge in Tesla Inc.’s stock on Thursday is turning into a major payday for its short sellers.

items.title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Surge
items.description: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high.

items.title: NYC’s Former HSBC Tower Seeks More Than $800 Million in Sale
items.description: A Manhattan office tower where Amazon.com Inc. has offices is going up for sale.

items.title: How Leveraged Exchange-Traded Funds Are Suddenly Everywhere
items.description: On this episode of Trillions, we discuss what’s fueling the boom and how these exchange-traded funds work in today’s market.

items.title: Dollar Rises as Intensifying Iran War Lifts Rate Hike Bets
items.description: The dollar jumped Thursday as intensifying conflict in the Middle East sparked concerns over energy supply disruptions, fueling expectations that interest rates will stay higher for longer.

items.title: S&P 500 Suffers Worst Day in a Month as Oil Soars Over $100
items.description: US stocks tumbled, with the S&P 500 Index suffering its worst day in a month, as doubts about whether the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence investment boom will pay off dragged down big tech stocks.

items.title: Bund Yields Hit 15-Year High as $100 Oil Fans Inflation Concerns
items.description: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging oil prices prompted bigger bets on interest-rate hikes, even as the European Central Bank kept its benchmark unchanged.

items.title: China Tankers Pass Red Sea Chokepoint Despite Houthi Threat
items.description: Two China-owned oil tankers loaded with Saudi Arabian crude exited the Red Sea via the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint despite overnight Houthi attacks on vessels in the region.

items.title: Yen’s Slump Extends Beyond Dollar and Stokes Inflation Fears
items.description: While the yen’s plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the Japanese currency’s health is also flashing a warning.

items.title: Gold Falls as Middle East Conflict Reverses Dip-Buying Momentum
items.description: Gold fell as escalating conflict in the Middle East continued to push up energy prices, fueling bets the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

items.title: Stocks Hit by AI and War Jitters as Oil Tops $100: Markets Wrap
items.description: A surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent stocks and bonds lower, with Wall Street also rattled by renewed concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will pay off.
